topic: Practical OpenAI and ChatGPT usage patterns, real use cases

entries.q: What is the OpenAI Cookbook and where can I access it
entries.a: The OpenAI Cookbook is a resource for example code and guides on using the OpenAI API, accessible at cookbook.openai.com

entries.q: What do I need to run the examples in the OpenAI Cookbook
entries.a: To run the examples, you need an OpenAI account and an associated API key, which can be set as an environment variable or in an .env file

entries.q: How can I set my OpenAI API key for use with the examples
entries.a: You can set your OpenAI API key as an environment variable called OPENAI_API_KEY or in an .env file at the root of your repository

entries.q: In what programming language are most OpenAI Cookbook code examples written
entries.a: Most code examples in the OpenAI Cookbook are written in Python, but the concepts can be applied in any language

entries.q: What type of license does the OpenAI Cookbook use
entries.a: The OpenAI Cookbook uses the MIT License

entries.q: Where can I find additional resources for learning about OpenAI and the OpenAI API
entries.a: Additional resources, including guides and courses, can be found on the OpenAI Cookbook website under related resources

entries.q: How do I create a free OpenAI account to get started with the OpenAI API
entries.a: You can create a free OpenAI account by signing up at platform.openai.com/signup
